turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough that just won't go away, and it's really started to affect my daily life.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: It's been a dry, hacking cough at first, but over the past few days it's changed to a productive cough. It's really annoying and makes me feel like I'm always clearing my throat.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: I'd say it started about two weeks ago, maybe a bit longer? At first, it was just this dry cough that wouldn't quit, but then it turned into something else and it's been getting worse ever since.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all the time now - throughout the day, every hour or so. Sometimes I'll go hours without coughing, but when it comes back, it's like nothing changes... it just keeps going and going!

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's definitely an 8 out of 10 in terms of severity. I'm feeling really exhausted all the time and having trouble sleeping.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing some difficulty breathing and wheezing sounds besides the cough. It's a bit alarming to me because I'm not used to feeling like that before.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It gets worse for sure when I try to talk or laugh, but even just deep breathing makes my chest feel tighter and the coughing worse.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been having some low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's intermittent and usually gets worse in the evenings. Sometimes I'll feel a bit chilly.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Honestly, it's just been this constant tightness in my chest, especially when I cough or take deep breaths.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, no swelling at all.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my symptoms with some over-the-counter dextromethorphan for cough suppression, but it hasn't really provided much relief.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I don't have any chronic medical conditions. Just seasonal allergies that I manage with cetirizine when they act up.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I've been feeling really tired lately and my cough is making it hard for me to focus on work and daily activities.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I actually don't smoke at all, but I do have a social drink habit - maybe one or two glasses of wine on weekends.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: I've been trying to eat pretty normally lately, but I have noticed that my appetite has been decreasing over the past few days. As for exercise, I'm an elementary school teacher, so I'm pretty active during the day.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, my mother actually has asthma.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My cough is making it hard for me to focus on work and daily activities. To be honest, I'm getting a bit worried about what's going on with my body - this cough just won't quit.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: My last physical exam was about a year ago, everything was normal back then.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I've been trying to take care of myself, but it's hard when I'm constantly feeling exhausted and my cough won't go away. It's affecting my daily life, which is really frustrating for me.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been having some night sweats, actually, which can be pretty alarming at first when they start happening every night. And I'm feeling really tired and not myself lately.
